body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
irrlicht_observes: yes

## agent_supports: yes

Codex's reset command is `/new`: it starts a fresh conversation that SUPERSEDES the current one, without relaunching the binary. The fresh session's rollout (`~/.codex/sessions/.../rollout-<ts>-<uuid2>.jsonl`, a distinct `session_meta.id`) materializes lazily on the first post-reset prompt and contains none of the pre-reset history. (`/clear` produces the same observable supersession and is what the committed recording was driven with; both rotate to a brand-new rollout UUID.)

## irrlicht_observes: yes

Reset for codex is supersession: a new session replaces the old one, and that IS the expected, correct behavior. The daemon (codex Source = FilesUnderRoot watching ~/.codex/sessions — adapter.go) detects the new rollout the instant it appears and emits transcript_new + a state_transition to ready + pid_discovered under a session_id never seen before. That fresh session_id appearing in ready is the complete, user-observable reset signal — exactly the scenario's assertion 'a new session row appears with a different session_id in ready'.

The committed recording captures the full arc: proc-<PID> pre-session → first rollout (UUID-1) runs a turn → reset → second rollout (UUID-2, fresh session_id) runs a turn. expected.jsonl encodes it as 8 phases and validates 8/8.

## Why there is no old_session_ended phase (and why that is correct)

Unlike claudecode — whose PIDManager same-PID cleanup emits a synchronized KindTranscriptRemoved for the rotated-out UUID at reset time (issue #169 / commit 56c4667) — codex emits NO removal event at reset. The old rollout simply lingers on disk, frozen, until the normal lifecycle cleans it up (process_exited on codex exit, or the readyTTL idle sweep; in the recording the old rollout's transcript_removed lands ~9.8 s later via that sweep, untethered from the reset).

This is NOT an observability gap. A synchronized old-session-removal is a claudecode implementation detail, not a universal requirement. Demanding codex emit one would be holding it to another adapter's event shape. Codex's contract is 'new supersedes old', the daemon fully observes the new session, so the scenario is satisfied — irrlicht_observes: yes.
caveats: Codex's reset command is /new; the committed recording was driven via the reset_session step (which sends /clear) — both produce the same observable: the old rollout freezes and a fresh rollout with a new session_id appears. Switching the driver's reset_session to /new for label fidelity is an optional follow-up that would need a re-record.

Codex emits no synchronized transcript_removed at reset (the old rollout lingers, swept later by idle/exit). This is expected supersession behavior, not a limitation — hence no old_session_ended phase, by design.

sources.note: Codex CLI slash commands — /new starts a fresh chat (session reset).
Source = FilesUnderRoot watching ~/.codex/sessions — any new rollout under the root is detected, so a reset-spawned new session is daemon-observable as a fresh session_id.
DiscoverPID binds the codex PID via transcript-writer; both pre- and post-reset sessions share one PID.
Committed recording: two distinct rollout session_ids (UUID-1, UUID-2) + proc-PID pre-session; expected.jsonl validates 8/8.
Reset command is /new; new-replaces-old is expected behavior — absent reset-time removal is not a gap.
